title: Plug Power Stock Is Running Hot After a Revenue Beat. The Hard Part Starts Now
text: Plug Power delivered first-quarter revenue of $163.5 million, representing 22% year-over-year growth. The hydrogen equipment manufacturer's stock jumped 11.2% Wednesday to $3.96 before settling at $3.86 Thursday. Despite the revenue beat, challenges persist. The company reported a net loss of $245.3 million, though loss per share improved to 18 cents from 21 cents due to share dilution. Gross margin, while still negative at -13%, showed significant improvement from -55% a year earlier. New CEO Jose Luis Crespo characterized the quarter as demonstrating strong commercial execution. The company maintains its target for positive EBITDAS by Q4 2026. Analysts remain divided. H.C. Wainwright maintained its buy rating with a $7 price target, while BMO assigned an underperform rating at $1.20. The stock carries substantial short interest—approximately 25% of tradable float—suggesting ongoing skepticism about whether Plug's turnaround is genuine. With $223.2 million in unrestricted cash available, management must now prove it can convert improved unit economics into sustainable cash flow generation.
